body.title: [sprint] Consequence check: JSON envelope principle
body.intention: I intend to verify that JSON envelope adoption produced measurable cost reduction with no regressions in DS tool parsing.
body.description:
**What changed:** All agent-to-agent inference responses and slate files now use JSON envelopes; ProseVersion carried forward when already present.
**Predicted unintended effects:** (1) Models that don't reliably emit valid JSON break the parser silently — check for fallback coverage. (2) Slate migration may break older tooling that reads prose format. (3) DS may emit JSON-looking prose that's not actually parsed — check toolloop.py still works with non-compliant models.
**Signals to watch:** DS error rate post-deploy; token counts in cost_usd per ticket (T-cost-to-close-metric); any 'DONE:' grep hits reappearing in toolloop.py.
**Gate condition:** Check by 2026-06-26 or when T-json-envelope-inference closes.

**Affected files:** None — observation only
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Observe predicted effects; annotate as occurred/clear/partial and close
**Completion criteria:** Annotated with outcome and closed.
